INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_CARAVAN_GUAR.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_CARAVAN_GUAR.text:
Sigh... Poor Arepas. That's no way for anyone
to go.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_CARAVAN_GUAR.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_CARAVAN_GUAR.text:
Get back to Colcannon and let Brother Migas know
what happened. Two deaths in one day, not to mention the
poor sheep... God have mercy.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_CARAVAN_GUAR.speaker: Fighter Shank
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_CARAVAN_GUAR.text:
You couldn't save her?! Damn! Damn!!
At least you busted 'em up good.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: {ACTOR_2}
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text: Did the trader from the capital pass through here yet?

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: Fighter Shank
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text:
Arepas? Sure, she came past earlier this morning.
Why do you ask?

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text: I would have expected her in town well before now.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: Fighter Shank
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text: So she's pokier than usual. Big deal.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text: Killed?! A shepherd and all the sheep?

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: Fighter Shank
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text:
Holy hell...! By a bunch of hogs?
...I got friends who are hogs...

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text:
Find Arepas, and fast. If someone's hurt her,
we'll make sure they don't try to escape through here.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: Fighter Shank
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text:
You sure we can't go with them? I'd feel better
busting some skulls than just hanging
around here.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_AFTER_PASTURE.text: We've got our job, they've got theirs.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_LAZ.speaker: Fighter Shank
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_LAZ.text: Well, well, well. Look what the cat dragged in.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_CON.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_CON.text: Blessings upon you, and good luck with the patrols.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_04.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_04.text: Blessings upon you, and good luck with the patrols.

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_03.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_03.text:
And all we do is stand here and watch this mountain pass
for twelve hours a day?

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_02.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_02.text: You know we're beadles, too, right?

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_01.speaker: Knight Flambé
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_01.text:
All's well here.
Aren't you supposed to be patrolling the pastures?

INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_01.speaker: Fighter Shank
INTRO_MOUNTAIN_GUARDS_BEFORE_PASTURE_01.text:
Yeah, what if some slimes roll up to a sheep and
give it a big, goopy kiss? Har har!

SHEPHERD_INTRO_NORTHWEST.speaker: {ACTOR_1}
SHEPHERD_INTRO_NORTHWEST.text: Poor guy... Better let Brother Migas know abou this.

CARAVAN_TRADER_CORPSE.speaker: {ACTOR_1}
CARAVAN_TRADER_CORPSE.text:
Better report back to Brother Migas.
Maybe he'll know what those creeps were talking about.

CARAVAN_TRADER.speaker: {ACTOR_2}
CARAVAN_TRADER.text:
Poor Arepas... Who were those creeps, anyway?
Think they're working with those hogs from before?

CARAVAN_TRADER.speaker: {ACTOR_3}
CARAVAN_TRADER.text: And what was all that about looking for God?

CARAVAN_TRADER.speaker: {ACTOR_1}
CARAVAN_TRADER.text:
Better report back to Brother Migas.
Maybe he'll know what they were talking about.

CARAVAN_CARRION_EATERS_05.text:
She wouldn't tell us.
Will you tell us?

CARAVAN_CARRION_EATERS_02.text: Do you know where God is?

CARAVAN_CARRION_EATERS_01.speaker: {ACTOR_2}
CARAVAN_CARRION_EATERS_01.text:
That's Arepas! Hey, you!
Step away from her!
She's gone. But you needn't mourn her.

She's with God now. Or she would be, if we knew where
your God was.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN_CONT.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN_CONT.text:
Would you check in with the beadles stationed over at
the eastern mountain pass? Find out if they've seen her.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.text:
The beadles are back! Yipee.
Another triumphant patrol, I assume?
{ACTOR_1} explains what happened in the forest pasture.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.text:
That's... 
I'm sure you did all you could.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.text:
I'll round up a few beadles to go give them a
proper burial.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.text: In the meantime, we may have some more bad news.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.text:
Arepas, the trader who drags herself all the way
out to a backwater like Colcannon, is late.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.text:
She should have been here a couple days ago at the latest.
Normally, I'd chalk it up to a general lack of enthusiasm
for the deep, drawling, drooling countryside...

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_CARAVAN.text:
...But what you've just witnessed has me worried.
Would you check in with the beadles stationed over at
the eastern mountain pass? Find out if they've seen her.

SHEPHERD_INTRO_EAST.text:
Trouble? Trouble?!
You think I'd be asleep if there were trouble?!
Are all beadles nowadays as thick as you?

Knocking around a bunch of goopy slimes and
disturbing a poor shepherd at his sleep.
Real important job you got yourself, there.

SHEPHERD_INTRO_SOUTH_CONT.text:
If you're heading to the pasture in the forest clearing,
have a care. Those weird egg guys have been getting
rowdier than usual.

SHEPHERD_INTRO_SOUTH.text:
Nope. Not unless you count a few wobbly slimes.
One good smack with the 'ole crook and they
cleared right out.

If you're heading to the pasture in the forest clearing,
have a care. Those weird egg guys have been getting
rowdier than usual.

CHEMIST_INTRO.text:
Ever felt the need to use the same item twice
in a single fight?

If so, consider bringing a Chemist with you.
We can duplicate items mid-combat.

SNOOPING_IMP.text:
Yowza! You scared me, sneaking up on me like that!
Yeesh!

What's that? Do I live here? Nope. I'm just snooping around.
How about you?

I guess we're both just a couple of snoops, eh?
Well, it's nice to meet a kindred spirit.

BAD_COOK_A.text:
My husband cooks the worst food I've ever eaten.
It tastes inconceivably bad.

MARTYR_INTRO.text:
When I meet someone new, I'm always curious to know
what makes them happiest in life.

It can vary dramatically from person to person. Good food...
True love... Inflicting pain... I've heard it all.

But for me, true happiness is...
Well. Take what's in the chest behind me.
I hope you'll understand.

ITEM_REMINDER.text:
At first, I was surprised that I could only hold one
of each item, but then I noticed my items refilled
at the end of combat.

I guess that means this one pot of cooking oil's gonna
last me a lifetime!

KNIGHT_INTRO.text:
As a knight, I just can't imagine giving up my shield
for a two-handed sword.

My husband says that makes me small-minded. I mean,
I guess it does. I should try to at least conceive of life
without a shield. For his sake.
It's no good. I can't do it.

FIGHTER_INTRO.text:
Weapons are important for a fighter! But it can be tough
to choose what to equip in each hand.

If I hold a sword in one hand a hammer in the other, then I can
use both sword and hammer skills...

But if I hold two hammers I'll deal more damage! Of course,
I'll only be able to use hammer skills...

INKEEPER_INTRO_TOWN_YES_MONEY.text: You're light. Get a few more CARO and come back.

INKEEPER_INTRO_TOWN.text:
Welcome to the inn.
It's 10 CARO for the night.

CULTIVATOR_INTRO.text:
If you can perceive of me, then you must be
a fellow cultivator.

See this resplendent peach? It's quite an auspicious thing.
Try consuming the next one you see in the wild.

The powers of the natural world shall course through
your body and fill your internal vessels with stores of
elemental energy.

With each attack, your body and weapon will channel
that elemental energy to produce profound effects.
Water heals you and your allies for a small amount.
Wood restores your own health considerably.
Fire increases your chance to hit enemy weak points.

Earth deals extra damage to the enemy.
The healthier they are, the more damage is inflicted.
Metal deals a small amount of damage to all enemies.

Ahh...! Truly, this peach is a wondrous thing.
Heed my advice and eat them without hesitation.

Not this one, though. It's mine.
You can't have it.

FARMER_INTRO.text:
Make sure to pick up any seeds you find lying around
on the ground. You'll never use them up,
so more seeds just means more types of crops.

Once your crops mature, they'll stick around until
you harvest them, so long as you don't plant
more than three of them.

They'll even stay good between battles, so don't fuss
that you need to use them as soon as they're ready.

Farming's all about planning and patience. Don't think about
what you need right this moment; think about what
you'll need, oh, say...

BUY_EQUIPMENT_REMINDER.text:
If you're heading outside, make sure you stop by the
stores here in town and stock up.

Fighting is a lot tougher when all you've got
is your slappers.

INTRO_NEARBY_ENEMIES.text:
Boy, I'm glad you beadles are here to help take
care of all those creeps roaming around outside
of town!

Nearby it's mostly slimes.
Individually, they're not much to write home about.

But let them gang up on you and your mama
better break out her cheaters.

HOMESICK_SLIME.text:
I should write home to my mama.
Let her know how I'm doing.

NEW_PLAYER_INTRO_SMALLTALK.speaker: Brother Polenta
NEW_PLAYER_INTRO_SMALLTALK.text:
Bless you for your work patrolling
the pastures. It may not be exciting
but it's important. The people
rely on you.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_PATROL.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_PATROL.text:
Blessings and all that. Been out to visit
the pastures yet?

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_PATROL.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_PATROL.text:
Daily patrols may not be glamorous,
but at least they're... Oh, Who am I
fooling? They're insufferable.

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_PATROL.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_PATROL.text: Good thing they're your job, not mine!

INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_PATROL.speaker: Brother Migas
INTRO_QUEST_CLERIC_PATROL.text:
...I guess I shouldn't say that, as a
cleric and all. 
Have fun!

NEW_PLAYER_INTRO.speaker: Brother Polenta
NEW_PLAYER_INTRO.text:
All beadles are welcome here. Speaking of,
have you had a chance to meet any fellow beadles yet?

NEW_PLAYER_INTRO.speaker: Brother Polenta
NEW_PLAYER_INTRO.text:
There are always brave folk willing to lay down
their lives to help those less fortunate.

NEW_PLAYER_INTRO.speaker: Brother Polenta
NEW_PLAYER_INTRO.text:
Sister Dal in the chancel above should
be able to introduce you to many
such beadles. Pray speak with her
before you leave.
